```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  mu <- sample(seq(-3,5,0.5),1); sigma <- sample(seq(0.5,4,0.5),1)
  k <- sample(seq(1.5,4,0.5),1); bound <- 1/k^2
  vals <- round(c(bound,1/k,1-bound,k^2,sigma^2/k^2),4)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Uma variável tem média `r mu` e desvio-padrão `r sigma`. Pelo limite de Chebyshev, qual é o limite superior para $P(|X-\mu|\geq`r k`\sigma)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Chebyshev afirma
$$P(|X-\mu|\geq k\sigma)\leq\frac1{k^2}
=`r sprintf("%.4f",bound)`.$$
O limite independe dos valores específicos de $\mu$ e $\sigma$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S10Q4_desigualdade_Chebyshev
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
